description: Vertical bar chart of the share of North Carolina students at each 2025 achievement level, all subjects and grades, in natural Level 1-or-2 to Level 5 order and labeled directly: Level 1 or 2 45%, Level 3 20%, Level 4 24%, Level 5 11%. The 45% scoring Level 1 or 2 — below grade level — exceeds the 35% reaching Levels 4 and 5 combined; Level 1 or 2 is highlighted rust to mark its below-grade-level valence and the others are neutral grey.

title.text: More NC students score Level 1 or 2 than Levels 4 and 5 combined
subtitle: Share of NC students by 2025 achievement level, all subjects and grades. Level 3+ = grade-level proficient (GLP);
Level 4+ = college-and-career ready (CCR). The below-grade-level bar is highlighted.

usermeta.source: Source: NCDPI accountability test scores (achievement levels, all subjects and grades, all students), 2025. Style-guide demo.
